methods.description: Analysis of sediment grain size distribution and mineralogical composition.

steps.description: About 150-250g of sediment was submitted to a grain size analysis by separating the fine fraction by wet sieving it using a 63μm screen
steps.searchable_summary: wet sieve sediment sample to separate fine fraction (<63μm)

steps.description: sieving the dry coarser fraction through a battery of sieves (125μm, 250μm, 500μm, 1000μm and 2000μm).
steps.searchable_summary: dry sieve coarser sediment fraction (>63μm) using specified sieve sizes

steps.description: The percentage of each sediment fraction was determined
steps.searchable_summary: calculate percentage of sediment in each size fraction
inputs: mass/volume of sediment in each size fraction

steps.description: the mean grain size of the sediment was evaluated using the Folk and Ward [35] method.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean grain size using Folk and Ward method

steps.description: The sediment fraction <63μm was submitted to a mineralogical analysis performed by Xray diffraction (XRD) techniques following the procedures described by Martins et al. [36], with the aim being to semi-quantify the pyrite, calcite and opal C/T contents.
steps.searchable_summary: perform X-ray diffraction (XRD) on fine sediment fraction to semi-quantify mineral content (pyrite, calcite, opal C/T)
outputs: semi-quantified mineral content (pyrite, calcite, opal C/T)

methods.description: Analysis of total and available elemental concentrations, Total Organic Carbon (TOC), and Sulfur (S) in the fine sediment fraction.

steps.description: The total elemental concentrations of Ca, As, Cd, Co, Cr, Cs, Cu, Hg, Ni, Pb, S and Zn was determined by ICP-MS after total digestion of the sediment with four acids (HClO 4 -HNO 3 -HCl-HF) at the ACME Analytical Laboratories (Canada).
steps.searchable_summary: perform total acid digestion (HClO4-HNO3-HCl-HF) on fine sediment fraction and analyze elemental concentrations (Ca, As, Cd, Co, Cr, Cs, Cu, Hg, Ni, Pb, S, Zn) using ICP-MS
outputs: total elemental concentrations (Ca, As, Cd, Co, Cr, Cs, Cu, Hg, Ni, Pb, S, Zn)

steps.description: The enrichment of the toxic chemical elements considered (As, Cd, Co, Cr, Cs, Cu, Ni, Pb and Zn) was calculated using the PLI of Tomlinson et al. [37], according to the adapted methodology followed by Martins et al. [1].
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Pollution Load Index (PLI) for toxic elements (As, Cd, Co, Cr, Cs, Cu, Ni, Pb, Zn) using Tomlinson et al. method
inputs: total elemental concentrations (As, Cd, Co, Cr, Cs, Cu, Ni, Pb, Zn)

steps.description: The available concentrations of As, Cd, Co, Cr, Cs, Cu, Hg, Ni, Pb and Zn, as well as Ca, were evaluated in the fine fraction with chemical fractionation methods followed by an ICP-MS analysis in the same lab. The sequential chemical extractions took place according to the following phases: Step 1 (S1: SLE): a 1M ammonium acetate leach for exchangeable cations adsorbed by clay and elements co-precipitated with carbonates; Step 2 (S2: SLO): a 0.1M sodium pyrophosphate leach for elements adsorbed by organic matter (humic and fulvic compounds); and Step 3 (S3: SLM): a 0.1M hydroxylamine leach for elements adsorbed by amorphous Mn hydroxide.
steps.searchable_summary: perform sequential chemical extraction (S1: 1M ammonium acetate, S2: 0.1M sodium pyrophosphate, S3: 0.1M hydroxylamine) on fine sediment fraction and analyze elemental concentrations (As, Cd, Co, Cr, Cs, Cu, Hg, Ni, Pb, Zn, Ca) in each extract using ICP-MS

steps.description: The total available concentrations (TAC) were evaluated by adding together the determined concentrations in each extracted phase (TAC = S1 + S2 + S3).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Total Available Concentrations (TAC) by summing concentrations from sequential extraction steps (S1+S2+S3)

steps.description: The TOC and S content were analyzed in the fine fraction with a LECO SC 144 device after acidification to remove carbonates according to [38] and [39] methodologies.
steps.searchable_summary: acidify fine sediment fraction to remove carbonates and analyze TOC and Sulfur content using LECO SC 144 device

steps.description: The ratio C/S was determined based on the LECO values.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Carbon to Sulfur (C/S) ratio

methods.description: Extraction and analysis of lipid, protein, and carbohydrate content in total sediment, including calculation of total biopolymers and ratios.

steps.description: The lipid (LIP) contents were extracted with chloroform-methanol according to Bligh and Dyer [40] and Marsh and Weinstein [41].
steps.searchable_summary: extract lipid content from total sediment using chloroform-methanol (Bligh and Dyer, Marsh and Weinstein methods)

steps.description: The results were compared with standards that are equivalent to tripalmitate.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze lipid extract and quantify content by comparison with tripalmitate standards

steps.description: The protein (PTN) analyses were carried out after extraction with NaOH (0.5M, 4h)
steps.searchable_summary: extract protein content from total sediment using NaOH (0.5M, 4h)

steps.description: and were determined according to Hartree [42], as modified by Rice [43], to compensate for phenol interference. The concentrations are reported as albumin equivalents.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze protein extract using modified Hartree method (Rice modification) and report as albumin equivalents

steps.description: The carbohydrate (CHO) content was determined according to Gerchacov and Hachter [44] and expressed as glucose equivalents. The method is based on the same principle as the widely-used approach of Dubois et al. [45], but is specifically adapted for CHO determination in sediment.
steps.searchable_summary: determine carbohydrate content using Gerchacov and Hachter method (adapted from Dubois et al.) and express as glucose equivalents

steps.description: For each biochemical analysis, blanks were made with the same sediment samples that had previously been treated in a muffle furnace (450°C, 2 h).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare blanks for biochemical analysis using muffle-furnaced sediment (450°C, 2h)

steps.description: All of the analyses were carried out in 3-5 replicates.
steps.searchable_summary: perform all biochemical analyses in 3-5 replicates

steps.description: The total concentrations of PTN, CHO and LIP were estimated and referred to as total biopolymers (TBP).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Total Biopolymers (TBP) by summing PTN, CHO, and LIP content

steps.description: The relative enrichment of the biopolymers was acessesed through the following ratios: PTN/CHO+LIP, LIP/CHO+PTN and CHO/PTN+LIP.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate biopolymer ratios: PTN/(CHO+LIP), LIP/(CHO+PTN), CHO/(PTN+LIP)

methods.description: Collection of sediment and water samples from intertidal areas and measurement of in-situ environmental parameters.

steps.description: Samples were obtained in summer of 2011 at water depths varying between 0.5m and~2m ( Fig. 1; S1 Appendix). The sediment samples were collected using an adapted Petit Ponnar sampler (with two openings, i.e. open at its upper and bottom parts, with the aim being for it to operate like a box-corer). The uppermost first centimeter of the undisturbed sediment (about 50ml) was stored...
steps.searchable_summary: collect uppermost 1 cm (~50ml) of undisturbed sediment using an adapted Petit Ponnar sampler at specified sites and depths (0.5-2m)

steps.description: ...stored in buffered ethanol stained with Rose Bengal (2g of Rose Bengal in 1000ml alcohol) to differentiate living from dead foraminifera [34].
steps.searchable_summary: store an aliquot of sediment sample in buffered ethanol stained with Rose Bengal

steps.description: Another aliquot of sediment was used for geochemical, mineralogical and grain size analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: store another aliquot of sediment sample for geochemical, mineralogical, and grain size analyses

steps.description: The physicochemical parameters of temperature and salinity/conductivity in water, and pH and Eh in sediment were measured at each site.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water temperature, salinity/conductivity, and sediment pH, Eh at each sampling site

methods.description: Preparation and analysis of sediment samples to identify benthic foraminiferal species, determine their abundance, and calculate community diversity.

steps.description: Process sediment aliquot stored in buffered ethanol stained with Rose Bengal (from sampling step) to differentiate living from dead foraminifera [34].
steps.searchable_summary: process stained sediment aliquot to separate and differentiate living and dead foraminifera

steps.description: A total of 76 benthic foraminiferal species were identified (S2 Appendix).
steps.searchable_summary: identify benthic foraminiferal species in processed samples

steps.description: The relative abundances of 23 species used for the statistical analyses herein are presented in S1 Appendix...
steps.searchable_summary: determine relative abundances of identified foraminiferal species

steps.description: ...together with details of foraminiferal density...
steps.searchable_summary: calculate foraminiferal density (ind/g)

steps.description: ...the Shannon index values...
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Shannon index for foraminiferal community diversity
